markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Implement pins, favorites, unlinked blocks, and sorted views with backend state. This MT belongs to LoomObsidianNavigation. Deliver the Obsidian replacement surface as a Handshake-native Loom projection.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Obsidian-like behavior must be implemented as Loom/project knowledge projections over PostgreSQL, not a markdown-vault clone.
Backlinks, graph, tags, folders, pins, color labels, and project links must have backend navigation APIs and visual-debug evidence.
scope.expected_behavior: PinsFavoritesAndUnlinked leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: PinsFavoritesAndUnlinked is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence.summary: Reorderable Pins grid + Unlinked triage backend for the four Loom views. The All/Unlinked/Sorted/Pins view queries were already built; MT-183 adds user-controlled pin ordering (the reorderable grid, [LM-VIEW-004]) and deterministic ordering.
files_changed: src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs - map_loom_block reads pin_order tolerantly (try_get) so the ~26 existing block SELECTs are unaffected; Pins view ORDER BY pin_order ASC NULLS LAST, updated_at DESC, block_id ASC; All/Unlinked get block_id tiebreaker; set_loom_block_pin_order (sets OR clears to NULL); update_loom_block patches pin_order; get_loom_block returns it

evidence.command: cargo test -p handshake_core --features runtime-full,test-utils --test loom_pins_views_tests  &&  --test storage_conformance  &&  --lib storage::tests:: -- --test-threads=1
evidence.result: pins/views ok. 4 passed; storage_conformance ok. 3 passed (incl postgres_loom_storage_conformance); storage::tests ok. 25 passed; 0 failed (real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL)
tests: pins_view_respects_user_pin_order - pin_order C=0,A=1,B=2 -> order C,A,B; reorder B=-1 -> B,C,A; pin_order returned on single read
clearing_pin_order_sends_block_to_the_end - clear B (NULL) -> NULLS LAST trails ordered A
unlinked_view_excludes_linked_blocks - lonely block unlinked; mention-linked src/tgt excluded
REGRESSION: postgres_loom_storage_conformance (four-views) + 25 loom storage tests still green after the select_blocks ORDER BY change

findings: NULL-CLEAR vs COALESCE (handled+tested): update_loom_block uses COALESCE so it can SET but not CLEAR pin_order; the dedicated set_loom_block_pin_order binds Option<i32> directly so NULL is a real clear. Both paths tested.

TOLERANT MAP (handled): map_loom_block reads pin_order via try_get().ok().flatten() so the ~26 existing block SELECTs that do not project the new column yield None instead of panicking. Conformance + storage tests confirm no regression.
ORDER-BY INJECTION (clear): the Pins/All/Unlinked order clauses are fixed &'static str literals threaded through select_blocks; no user input reaches ORDER BY.
DETERMINISM (handled+tested): Pins order is pin_order ASC NULLS LAST, updated_at DESC, block_id ASC (total order even with equal pin_order/updated_at). All/Unlinked got a block_id tiebreaker.
MIGRATION SAFETY (handled): 0293 is additive + nullable with a partial index; safe on the existing table, no backfill.
FAIL-CLOSED (tested): set_loom_block_pin_order returns NotFound for a missing block.
fixes: No code change required beyond the dedicated clear path; findings handled-by-design and tested.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
